title: INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, Upsert, and Mutation Evidence · Data Contract Brief: Update Scope
summary: A ticket import and reassignment workflow must change only intended rows and retain evidence of what happened. Planned artifact: Add and verify the sql update scope increment in a guarded ticket-mutation workflow. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Preview an UPDATE predicate as a SELECT and verify changed-row count and postconditions.
Insert validated rows from explicit values or a query while naming target columns.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.sql:
-- sql-basics cumulative relational data portfolio.
-- Each increment declares grain, predicts cardinality or changed rows, runs in fresh browser SQLite,
-- reconciles results, tests changed data, and records PostgreSQL or standard-SQL boundaries.

steps.title: Predict Update Scope · Theory Update Scope
steps.instruction:
theory case — Import ticket 202 twice, reassign one unowned ticket, reject an invalid severity, and migrate a required category field without breaking the old writer. Preview mutation scope and retain rollback plus changed-row evidence.
